records.claim: Polylogue can compare agent affordance usage across normalized action evidence without summing unlike tool-name spellings or provider-specific call shapes.
records.non_claim: This is not a human-quality utility evaluation of any particular tool family. It measures captured usage evidence, failure signals, and coverage gaps; usefulness still requires qualitative review of session context and outcomes.

records.claim: Polylogue can regenerate a current v24 longitudinal agent-usage forensics packet over the live archive using product analysis surfaces, with provenance-separated token/cost lanes and explicit physical-vs-logical token grains.
records.non_claim: This packet is not provider billing truth, not an LLM judgment of failure follow-up behavior, and not a resurrected standalone forensics script; it reads the current archive through product commands and points structured-failure follow-up to the current claim-vs-evidence packet.

records.claim: Polylogue can distinguish unresolved raw-materialization work from classified raw/index join gaps on the active archive, so operators do not keep replaying aliases or non-session artifacts as if they were missing transcript sessions.
records.non_claim: This is not a full raw-row forensic audit and it does not prove every classified source row is semantically valuable. It proves the current debt surface has no actionable/open raw-materialization work and keeps classified evidence inspectable.

records.claim: Polylogue can ground a failure-follow-up finding in normalized tool-result outcomes, state the bounded sample frame, and publish aggregate rates without exposing raw private transcripts.
records.non_claim: The live aggregate is not reproducible without the private archive; the deterministic demo archive reproduces the method and artifact shape, not the private corpus rates.

records.claim: Polylogue can produce a bounded claim-vs-evidence report by anchoring on structured tool failures and classifying the immediately following assistant turn for explicit failure acknowledgment.
records.non_claim: This is not a whole-archive rate unless --limit exceeds all structured failures, and it is not an LLM judgment of intent or utility. Ambiguous follow-ups remain in the denominator.

records.claim: Polylogue can emit a read-only lineage validation artifact that separates physical stored archive counts from logical session counts before those numbers are cited externally.
records.non_claim: This artifact does not prove every composed transcript is byte-identical to the pre-lineage archive; it samples composed reads and flags residual integrity gaps for follow-up.
